Hyperosmolar foods are those that contain high levels of sugar or other substances that draw water into the gut, such as fruit juices or sweets. When these foods are consumed, they can cause a rapid influx of fluid into the small bowel, leading to abdominal pain, bloating, diarrhea, nausea, and vomiting. This occurs because the body is unable to absorb the excess fluid and nutrients quickly enough, leading to an imbalance in the gut. Dumping syndrome can occur in people who have had certain types of stomach surgery, such as gastric bypass, as well as in those with certain medical conditions. Treatment may involve dietary modifications, medication, or in severe cases, surgery.
